verses.text: Sl 82 Deus condena os que maltratam o desamparado. 82.1 Deus preside e está pronto para julgar (Is 3.13). • corte celeste: cf. Jó 1.6.
82.2 Visto que a justiça marca o reinado de Deus (89.4), ele se opõe ao favorecimento dos ímpios. Por isso o salmista, perplexo, clama a Deus por justiça (82.3-4,8).

82:3 Defend the poor and fatherless,.... Or, judge (d) them; such as have no money to enter and carry on a suit, and have no friends to assist and advise them, and abide by them; these should be taken under the care and wing of judges; their cause should be attended to, and justice done them; their persons should be protected, and their property defended and secured for, since they are called gods, they ought to imitate him whose name they bear, who is the Father of the fatherless, the Judge of the widows, and the helper of the poor that commit themselves to him, Ps 10:14, such a righteous judge and good magistrate was Job;

82:4 Deliver the poor and needy,.... From his adversary and oppressor, who is mightier than he, and draws him to the judgment seat; when it is not in his power to defend himself against him, and get out of his hands, unless a righteous judge will show a regard to him and his cause; and sometimes even an unjust judge, through importunity, will do this, as everyone ought, and every righteous one will: rid them out of the hand of the wicked;
82.5 Os opressores injustos não podem trazer nem restaurar a ordem. Vagam nas trevas, imóveis diante das circunstâncias, mesmo quando toda a ordem do mundo desmorona à sua volta (cf. 11.3).
82.6-7 Os “deuses” (notas em 29.1-2; 58.1), como meros mortais, não podem escapar do juízo comum aos governantes humanos. Jesus citou esta passagem em Jo 10.34.
82.6-7 Os “deuses” (notas em 29.1-2; 58.1), como meros mortais, não podem escapar do juízo comum aos governantes humanos. Jesus citou esta passagem em Jo 10.34.
82.8 As nações pertencem a Deus; ele tomará posse dos domínios antes governados por divindades locais e os dará ao seu povo, como fez com a Terra Prometida.
